root_cause.text: User-controlled input (such as filenames) is included in HTTP headers without adequately sanitizing CRLF sequences, allowing attackers to manipulate the request by injecting arbitrary HTTP headers.
root_cause.evidence: The patch modifies the header value sanitization logic in `FileTransfer.java` by changing `headerValue.replaceAll("\\s+", "").replaceAll("\\n","")` to replace whitespace and newline characters with a safe space character (`" "`), ensuring that any injected CRLF sequences are properly neutralized.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: User-controlled input (such as filenames) is included in HTTP headers without adequately sanitizing CRLF sequences, allowing attackers to manipulate the request by injecting arbitrary HTTP headers.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch modifies the header value sanitization logic in `FileTransfer.java` by changing `headerValue.replaceAll("\\s+", "").replaceAll("\\n","")` to replace whitespace and newline characters with a safe space character (`" "`), ensuring that any injected CRLF sequences are properly neutralized.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix header sanitization logic to replace whitespace and newlines with spaces instead of removing them, preserving word boundaries.
intent_record.what: Changed the replacement string for whitespace and newline characters from an empty string to a single space, and removed an extra semicolon.
intent_record.how: Prevents adjacent text from merging incorrectly when whitespace or newlines are stripped during HTTP header sanitization.

grouping_result.intent: Fix header sanitization logic to replace whitespace and newlines with spaces instead of removing them, preserving word boundaries.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly modifies the header sanitization logic to properly handle newlines (CRLF), which is the exact mechanism needed to fix the CRLF injection vulnerability described in the root cause.

candidates.rationale: This line contains the faulty sanitization logic for HTTP header values, which fails to adequately handle CRLF sequences and allows header injection.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement contains the faulty sanitization logic for HTTP header values that incorrectly handles whitespace and newlines, allowing for CRLF injection.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Fixing headers so they don't accept non-ASCII
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit ad6647120db12f0e67ee4a952a71ea494a39a475
Author: Joe Bowser <bowserj@apache.org>
Date:   Tue Aug 18 14:55:18 2015 -0700

    Fixing headers so they don't accept non-ASCII

diff --git a/src/android/FileTransfer.java b/src/android/FileTransfer.java
index 9c8dacb..4baefe7 100644
--- a/src/android/FileTransfer.java
+++ b/src/android/FileTransfer.java
@@ -197,12 +197,32 @@ public class FileTransfer extends CordovaPlugin {
     private static void addHeadersToRequest(URLConnection connection, J
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces explicit sanitization of HTTP header keys and values by stripping out newlines and non-ASCII characters using `replaceAll("\\n", "")` and `replaceAll("[^\\x20-\\x7E]+", "")`. This fixes the CRLF injection vulnerability described in the root cause.
